volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 2.5.428c - Section 2.5.472b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 2.5.428c
sections.translation: No , neither of you .

sections.label: Section 2.5.428d
sections.translation: I very much fear —

sections.label: Section 2.5.429
sections.translation: What do you fear ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.429b
sections.original: Enim ne nos nosmet perdiderimus uspiam ;
sections.translation: Why , that we have lost ourselves somewhere or other ; for she says that she knows neither you nor me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.430b
sections.translation: I wish , Sceledrus , to examine into this , whether we are ourselves , or else some other persons ; lest secretly somehow some one of our neighbours may have transformed us without our knowing it .

sections.label: Section 2.5.433
sections.translation: For my part , beyond a doubt , I am my own self .

sections.label: Section 2.5.433b
sections.original: Et pol ego . quaeris tu , mulier , malum .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and so am I .

sections.label: Section 2.5.434
sections.translation: My lady , you are seeking your destruction . [*] To you I am speaking ; hark you , Philocomasium !

sections.label: Section 2.5.434b
sections.translation: What craziness possesses you ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.435
sections.original: qui me perperam perplexo nomine appelles ?
sections.translation: to be calling me wrongly by a crackjaw name [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.435b
sections.translation: How now ! What are you called , then ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.436b
sections.translation: My name is Glycera .

sections.label: Section 2.5.436c
sections.translation: For a bad purpose , Philocomasium , you wish to have a wrong name [*] . Away with you , shocking woman ; for most notably are you doing a wrong to my master .

sections.label: Section 2.5.439
sections.translation: I ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.439b
sections.translation: Yes , you .

sections.label: Section 2.5.439c
sections.original: Quaé heri Athenis Ephesum adveni vesperi
sections.translation: I , who arrived from Athens yesterday evening at Ephesus ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.440
sections.translation: with my lover , a young man of Athens ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.440b
sections.translation: Tell me ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.441
sections.original: quid hic tibi in Epheso est negoti ?
sections.translation: what business have you here in Ephesus ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.441b
sections.translation: I had heard that my own twin-sister is here in Ephesus ; I came here to look for her .

sections.label: Section 2.5.442b
sections.translation: You ’ re a good-for-nothing woman .

sections.label: Section 2.5.443
sections.original: Immo ecastor stulta multum , quae vobiscum fabuler .
sections.translation: Yes , i ’ faith , I am a very foolish one to be parleying with you fellows . I am going .

sections.label: Section 2.5.444b
sections.translation: I won ’ t let you go .

sections.label: Section 2.5.444c
sections.translation: Let me go .

sections.label: Section 2.5.444d
sections.translation: You are discovered in the fact .

sections.label: Section 2.5.445
sections.translation: I won ’ t let you go .

sections.label: Section 2.5.445b
sections.original: At iam crepabunt mihi manus , malae tibi ,
sections.translation: But my hands shall just now sound again against your cheek , if you don ’ t let me go .

sections.label: Section 2.5.446b
sections.original: Quid , malum , astas ? quin tenes altrinsecus ?
sections.translation: Why the plague are you standing idle ? Why don ’ t you hold her on the other side ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.447
sections.original: Nil moror negotiosum mi esse tergum . qui scio
sections.translation: I don ’ t choose to bring the business down upon my back . How do I know but that this is not Philocomasium , but is some other female that resembles her ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.449
sections.translation: Will you let me go , or will you not let me go ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.449b
sections.translation: No ; by force and against your will , in spite of you ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.450
sections.original: nisi voluntate ibis , rapiam te domum .
sections.translation: I ’ ll drag you home , unless you ’ ll go of your own accord .

sections.label: Section 2.5.450b
sections.translation: This is my lodging here abroad [*] , at Athens is my home .

sections.label: Section 2.5.452
sections.original: neque moror neque vos qui hómines sitis novi neque scio .
sections.translation: I have nothing to do with that house , nor do I know or understand yourselves what persons you are .

sections.label: Section 2.5.453
sections.original: Lege agito : te nusquam mittam , nisi das firmatam fidem ,
sections.translation: Proceed against me [*] at law . I ’ ll never let you go , until you give me your solemn word that you will go indoors here ( pointing to the CAPTAIN ’ S house ) if I let go of you .

sections.label: Section 2.5.454b
sections.translation: You are compelling me by force , whoever you are .

sections.label: Section 2.5.455
sections.original: do fidem , si omittis , isto me intro ituram quo iubes .
sections.translation: I give you my word , that if you let go of me , I will go into that house where you bid me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.456
sections.translation: Then , now I let go of you .

sections.label: Section 2.5.456b
sections.translation: And , as I ’ m let go , I ’ ll go in here .

sections.label: Section 2.5.456c
sections.translation: She has acted with a woman ’ s honour .

sections.label: Section 2.5.457
sections.original: Sceledre , manibus amisisti praedam . tám east quam potis
sections.translation: Sceledrus , you ’ ve lost the prey through your hands ; as sure as possible she is the lady of our master . Do you intend to act in this matter with spirit ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.459
sections.translation: How am I to act ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.459b
sections.translation: Bring me a sword out here from in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.5.459c
sections.translation: What will you do with it ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.460
sections.original: Intro rumpam recta in aedis : quemque hic intus videro
sections.translation: I ’ ll break right into the house [*] ; and whatever man I see in-doors there caressing Philocomasium , I ’ ll behead him on the spot .

sections.label: Section 2.5.462
sections.translation: And do you think that it was she ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.462b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it was she , sure enough .

sections.label: Section 2.5.462c
sections.translation: But how she did dissemble .

sections.label: Section 2.5.463b
sections.translation: Go , bring me a sword out here .

sections.label: Section 2.5.463c
sections.translation: I ’ ll have it here this moment .

sections.label: Section 2.5.464
sections.original: Neque eques neque pedes profectost quisquam tanta audacia ,
sections.translation: Beyond a doubt , neither any horse nor foot has so great a degree of boldness

sections.label: Section 2.5.465
sections.original: qui aeque faciat confidenter quicquam quam mulier facit .
sections.translation: in carrying out anything with as much confidence as some women . How cleverly and how skilfully she performed her part in both her characters !— how her wary keeper , my fellow-servant , is being gulled ! ’ Tis most fortunate that the passage communicates through the party-wall .

sections.label: Section 2.5.469
sections.translation: Hallo ! Palaestrio , there ’ s no occasion for the sword .

sections.label: Section 2.5.469b
sections.translation: How so ?— or what ’ s the matter now ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.470
sections.translation: Our master ’ s lady is there , at home .

sections.label: Section 2.5.470b
sections.translation: What ? At home ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.470c
sections.translation: She ’ s lying on the sofa .

sections.label: Section 2.5.471
sections.original: Edepol ne tu tibi malam rem repperisti , ut praedicas .
sections.translation: Faith , but you ’ ve certainly brought on yourself a disagreable affair , according to what you report .

sections.label: Section 2.5.472
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.472b
sections.original: Quia hanc attingere ausus esausu's mulierem hinc ex proxumo .
sections.translation: Inasmuch as you have dared to touch that lady next door here .
